info.title: Signalbase API
info.description: Complete Signalbase V2 API: signal endpoints (funding, acquisitions, job changes, hiring, investors), companies, CSV enrichment, and webhooks. Generated from the per-page specs under api-reference/*/openapi.json (run scripts/build-openapi.mjs).

get.description: Fetch funding signals with filtering, pagination, and search capabilities. Returns a list of companies with funding activity including round types, amounts, and investor information.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100)

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter signals from this date (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter signals up to this date (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: date_preset
parameters.description: Relative date shorthand. Takes precedence over dateFrom/dateTo when provided.

parameters.name: countries
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of country codes to filter by

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: Pipe-separated list of company categories / industries to filter by

parameters.name: round
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of funding round types to filter by. Exact, case-insensitive match ('Series A', 'series a', and 'SERIES A' all match). A value not in the accepted list returns 400 Bad Request. See the Enums page for the accepted values.

parameters.name: round_flavor
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of round flavors to filter by

parameters.name: subcategories
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of subcategory IDs to filter by. These filter companies based on their assigned subcategory.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by company name or industry keywords

parameters.name: industry
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of exact industry names to filter by

parameters.name: company_name
parameters.description: Search by company name (partial match)

parameters.name: company_domain
parameters.description: **Recommended.** Company website domain, e.g. `novartis.com`. Strict match — if no company has this domain the result is empty; never falls back to fuzzy name matching. Variants are equivalent: `novartis.com`, `www.novartis.com` and `https://novartis.com/` all match the same company. Malformed values return 400. Takes priority over `company_name`; combined identifiers must agree or the result is empty.

parameters.name: company_linkedin_url
parameters.description: **Recommended.** LinkedIn company URL, e.g. `linkedin.com/company/novartis`. Strict match — if no company has this LinkedIn page the result is empty; never falls back to fuzzy name matching. `http`/`https`, optional `www.` and trailing slash are equivalent. Non-company LinkedIn URLs (e.g. personal `/in/` profiles) return 400. Takes priority over `company_name`; combined identifiers must agree or the result is empty.

parameters.name: amount_min
parameters.description: Minimum funding amount in whole units of the deal's currency (not cents)

parameters.name: amount_max
parameters.description: Maximum funding amount in whole units of the deal's currency (not cents)

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Filter by currency, as an ISO 4217 code (e.g. USD, EUR, GBP). Most records are USD. Exact match.

parameters.name: verification_status
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of verification statuses to filter by

parameters.name: investor_name
parameters.description: Search by investor name (partial match)

parameters.name: employee_count_min
parameters.description: Minimum company employee count

parameters.name: employee_count_max
parameters.description: Maximum company employee count

parameters.name: founded_year_min
parameters.description: Minimum company founded year

parameters.name: founded_year_max
parameters.description: Maximum company founded year

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort results by

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort direction

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: When set to "true", returns only pagination metadata (totalCount) with an empty data array. No credits are charged.

data.companyDescription: dtcpay is a regulated payment service provider enabling seamless digital payments.

sources.title: dtcpay raises $10M in Series A led by Sequoia Capital

400.description: Bad request - an invalid filter value was supplied (e.g. an unrecognized `round` value)

example.error: Invalid round value: foobar. Accepted values (case-insensitive): pre-seed, seed, series a, series b, series c, series d, series e, series f, series g, series h, angel, grant, crowdfunding, growth, debt, debt financing, equity, fund, ipo.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

example.error: Invalid API key
out of credits, please contact support to increase your usage
Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.
An unknown error occurred

get.description: Fetch acquisition signals with filtering, pagination, and search capabilities. Returns acquisition events with acquired company details, acquiring company information, deal amounts, and source references.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100)

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter signals from this date (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter signals up to this date (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: date_preset
parameters.description: Relative date shorthand. Takes precedence over dateFrom/dateTo.

parameters.name: countries
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of country codes to filter by (acquired company)

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: Pipe-separated list of company categories / industries to filter by

parameters.name: subcategories
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of subcategory IDs to filter by

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by company name or industry keywords

parameters.name: industry
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of exact industry names to filter by

parameters.name: company_name
parameters.description: Search by acquired company name (partial match)

parameters.name: company_domain
parameters.description: **Recommended.** Company website domain, e.g. `novartis.com`. Strict match — if no company has this domain the result is empty; never falls back to fuzzy name matching. Variants are equivalent: `novartis.com`, `www.novartis.com` and `https://novartis.com/` all match the same company. Malformed values return 400. Takes priority over `company_name`; combined identifiers must agree or the result is empty.

parameters.name: company_linkedin_url
parameters.description: **Recommended.** LinkedIn company URL, e.g. `linkedin.com/company/novartis`. Strict match — if no company has this LinkedIn page the result is empty; never falls back to fuzzy name matching. `http`/`https`, optional `www.` and trailing slash are equivalent. Non-company LinkedIn URLs (e.g. personal `/in/` profiles) return 400. Takes priority over `company_name`; combined identifiers must agree or the result is empty.

parameters.name: acquiring_company
parameters.description: Search by acquiring company name (partial match)

parameters.name: amount_min
parameters.description: Minimum acquisition amount in whole USD (not cents)

parameters.name: amount_max
parameters.description: Maximum acquisition amount in whole USD (not cents)

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Filter by currency, as an ISO 4217 code. Acquisition amounts are stored as USD by convention (the vast majority); a few records are EUR. Exact match.

parameters.name: verification_status
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of verification statuses to filter by

parameters.name: employee_count_min
parameters.description: Minimum company employee count

parameters.name: employee_count_max
parameters.description: Maximum company employee count

parameters.name: founded_year_min
parameters.description: Minimum company founded year

parameters.name: founded_year_max
parameters.description: Maximum company founded year

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort results by

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort direction

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: When set to "true", returns only pagination metadata with an empty data array. No credits are charged.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

example.error: Invalid API key
out of credits, please contact support to increase your usage
Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.

get.description: Fetch job change signals with filtering, pagination, and search. Supports role-aware position filters, department and seniority filters, LinkedIn targeting, and field-specific search. Person names are GDPR-masked (first name + last initial only).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100)

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter signals from this date (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter signals up to this date (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: date_preset
parameters.description: Relative date shorthand. Takes precedence over dateFrom/dateTo.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Free-text search across company name, industry, person name, role, person location, and person country

parameters.name: countries
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of country codes to filter by (matches person country or company HQ)

parameters.name: city
parameters.description: Free-text search on person city and person location

parameters.name: personLinkedinUrl
parameters.description: Exact LinkedIn profile URL of the person

parameters.name: company_linkedin_url
parameters.description: **Recommended.** LinkedIn company URL, e.g. `linkedin.com/company/novartis`. Strict match — if no company has this LinkedIn page the result is empty; never falls back to fuzzy name matching. `http`/`https`, optional `www.` and trailing slash are equivalent. Non-company LinkedIn URLs (e.g. personal `/in/` profiles) return 400. Takes priority over `company_name`; combined identifiers must agree or the result is empty. (Legacy alias: `companyLinkedinUrl`.)

parameters.name: company_name
parameters.description: Search by company name (partial match)

parameters.name: company_domain
parameters.description: **Recommended.** Company website domain, e.g. `novartis.com`. Strict match — if no company has this domain the result is empty; never falls back to fuzzy name matching. Variants are equivalent: `novartis.com`, `www.novartis.com` and `https://novartis.com/` all match the same company. Malformed values return 400. Takes priority over `company_name`; combined identifiers must agree or the result is empty.

parameters.name: new_role
parameters.description: Search by new role / job title (partial match)

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Exact match on signal source

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.description: Search by keyword tag (partial match)

parameters.name: positions
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of positions to filter by (e.g., ceo, cto, cfo, coo, vp of engineering, head of product, engineering manager, product manager, founder, co-founder)

parameters.name: departments
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of departments to filter by (e.g., marketing, sales, engineering, product, design, operations, finance, people, data, customer_success, growth, legal)

parameters.name: seniorities
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of seniority levels to filter by (e.g., founder, c_level, vp, director, head, lead, manager)

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort by

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort direction

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: When set to "true", returns only pagination metadata with an empty data array. No credits are charged.

200.description: Successful response with job change signals

data.postContent: Excited to announce my new role as CTO at NextGen Software!
data.personCurrentTitle: Chief Technology Officer at NextGen Software
data.personHeadline: CTO | Building the future of enterprise software

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

example.error: Invalid API key
out of credits, please contact support to increase your usage
Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.

get.description: Fetch hiring signals (open job postings) with filtering, pagination, and search. Supports role-aware position filters, department and seniority filters, location targeting, team size, and applicant range filtering.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100)

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter signals from this date (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter signals up to this date (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: date_preset
parameters.description: Relative date shorthand. Takes precedence over dateFrom/dateTo.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Free-text search across company name, industry, job title, location, and city

parameters.name: countries
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of country codes to filter by

parameters.name: states
parameters.description: Comma-separated US state codes to filter by

parameters.name: city
parameters.description: Free-text search on hiring signal city, location, and region

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: Pipe-separated list of company industry categories to filter by

parameters.name: subcategories
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of subcategory IDs to filter by

parameters.name: positions
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of positions to filter by (e.g., ceo, cto, cfo, coo, vp of engineering, head of product, engineering manager, product manager, founder, co-founder)

parameters.name: departments
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of departments to filter by (e.g., marketing, sales, engineering, product, design, operations, finance, people, data, customer_success, growth, legal)

parameters.name: seniorities
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of seniority levels to filter by (e.g., founder, c_level, vp, director, head, lead, manager)

parameters.name: team_size
parameters.description: Comma-separated team size ranges (e.g., 1-10, 11-50, 51-200, 201-1000, 1000-plus)

parameters.name: applicants
parameters.description: Comma-separated applicant count ranges (e.g., 0-25, 26-50, 51-100, 101-200, 201-plus)

parameters.name: company_name
parameters.description: Search by company name (partial match, fuzzy). Prefer `company_domain` or `company_linkedin_url` for strict matching.

parameters.name: company_domain
parameters.description: **Recommended.** Company website domain, e.g. `novartis.com`. Strict match — if no company has this domain the result is empty; never falls back to fuzzy name matching. Variants are equivalent: `novartis.com`, `www.novartis.com` and `https://novartis.com/` all match the same company. Malformed values return 400. Takes priority over `company_name`; combined identifiers must agree or the result is empty.

parameters.name: company_linkedin_url
parameters.description: **Recommended.** LinkedIn company URL, e.g. `linkedin.com/company/novartis`. Strict match — if no company has this LinkedIn page the result is empty; never falls back to fuzzy name matching. `http`/`https`, optional `www.` and trailing slash are equivalent. Non-company LinkedIn URLs (e.g. personal `/in/` profiles) return 400. Takes priority over `company_name`; combined identifiers must agree or the result is empty.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort by

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort direction

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: When set to "true", returns only pagination metadata with an empty data array. No credits are charged.

data.title: Senior Software Engineer
data.descriptionText: We are looking for a senior software engineer...

get.description: Fetch investor data with filtering, pagination, and search. Returns venture capital firms, angel investors, PE firms, corporate investors, accelerators, family offices, hedge funds, and crowdfunding platforms.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100)

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Filter by created date from (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Filter by created date to (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: date_preset
parameters.description: Relative date shorthand. Takes precedence over dateFrom/dateTo.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Free-text search across investor name and type

parameters.name: countries
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of country codes to filter by (matches investor's countries JSON array)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of investor types to filter by. The underlying `type` data is free-text, so these values are convenience groupings matched case-insensitively against many real-world variants (e.g. `vc` matches "VC", "VC firm", "Venture Capital", "venture_capital", "Corporate VC"; `angel` matches "Angel", "Solo angel", "Angel network"). For anything outside this list, use the `search` parameter, which also matches the type field. Note: the `type` value in the response is the original scraped string (e.g. "Corporate VC"), not the slug you filtered by, so a single `type=vc` query returns rows with mixed `type` values.

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: Legacy: Pipe-separated list of investor types (same as type parameter)

parameters.name: headquarters
parameters.description: Search by headquarters location (partial match)

parameters.name: ticket_size_min
parameters.description: Minimum ticket size in USD

parameters.name: ticket_size_max
parameters.description: Maximum ticket size in USD

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort by

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort direction

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: When set to "true", returns only pagination metadata with an empty data array. No credits are charged.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

example.error: Invalid API key
out of credits, please contact support to increase your usage
Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.
An unknown error occurred

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100)

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Free-text search across company name, industry, description, keywords, and specialties

parameters.name: countries
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of country codes to filter by

parameters.name: industry
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of industry names to filter by (exact match)

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: Pipe-separated list of company industry categories to filter by

parameters.name: subcategories
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of subcategory IDs to filter by

parameters.name: employee_count_min
parameters.description: Minimum employee count

parameters.name: employee_count_max
parameters.description: Maximum employee count

parameters.name: founded_year_min
parameters.description: Minimum founded year

parameters.name: founded_year_max
parameters.description: Maximum founded year

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort by

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort direction

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: If set to true, returns only the total count without data rows and without deducting credits.

data.name: NextGen Software
data.description: Enterprise SaaS platform for workflow automation.

data.name: HealthTech Solutions

post.description: Submit companies for enrichment. Each company must include at least one identifier: company name, website URL/domain, or LinkedIn URL. The endpoint costs 1 credit per accepted job. Add wait=true for small lists to return completed results inline when processing finishes within timeout seconds.

200.description: Enrichment job accepted. If wait=true and processing completed, data also includes status, summary, and results.

get.description: Retrieve job status, progress, summary counts, and completed results. Polling is free and returns creditsUsed: 0. Results are paginated with cursor and limit.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: Job ID returned by POST /csv-enrichment

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Pagination cursor returned as data.nextCursor

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Results per page. Defaults to 50, maximum 100.

post.description: Create a webhook subscription for the authenticated team. The full signing secret is returned exactly once in this response — store it now, because every later read returns only a 4-character preview. Creating a webhook is free (0 API credits). A team may have at most 10 active webhooks.

201.description: Webhook created. data includes the full signing secret (shown only on create).

get.description: List the authenticated team's active webhook subscriptions, newest first. Secrets are never returned here — each item includes only secret_preview. Free (0 credits).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of webhooks to return. Minimum 1, maximum 100, default 100.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of webhooks to skip for pagination. Default 0.

200.description: Array of webhook subscriptions (secret preview only).

get.description: Retrieve a single active webhook by ID. Returns secret_preview only (never the full secret). Soft-deleted webhooks return 404. Free (0 credits).

put.description: Update any subset of a webhook's fields. Set rotate_secret to true to mint a new signing secret — the response then includes the full new secret (shown only on rotation) and the previous secret immediately stops verifying. Set active to true to re-enable a previously deleted webhook. Free (0 credits).

200.description: The updated webhook. Includes the full secret only when rotate_secret was true; otherwise secret_preview.

delete.description: Soft-delete a webhook by setting active to false. It stops receiving events and disappears from list/retrieve. The record and its delivery history are retained, and it can be re-enabled with PUT active:true. Free (0 credits).

post.description: Deliver a signed system.test event to the webhook's endpoint immediately, without waiting for a real signal, and record it as a delivery. The request body is ignored. Returns the delivery outcome. Free (0 credits).

200.description: The test event was sent and recorded as a delivery.

get.description: List delivery attempts for a webhook, newest first, using keyset (cursor) pagination. Optionally filter by status. Free (0 credits).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Deliveries per page. Minimum 1, maximum 100, default 50.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by delivery status.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned as pagination.next_cursor. Pass it back to fetch the next page. A null next_cursor means there are no more rows.

post.description: Replay a failed delivery inline. The retry reuses the original event_id and event_type (so receivers can dedupe) and re-signs the payload with the subscription's current secret. Retrying a delivery that already succeeded returns 409. A successful retry is billed as one delivery (1 credit); a failed retry is free.

parameters.name: deliveryId
parameters.description: ID of the delivery to retry (the id field from List Deliveries).

200.description: The retry was attempted. success indicates whether the replay reached the endpoint with a 2xx.

bearerAuth.description: Use your `ff_live_…` API key as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful

signalId.description: Unique identifier for the funding signal

match_confidence.description: How this row's company was matched: "exact" (via `company_domain` / `company_linkedin_url`), "fuzzy" (via `company_name` partial match), or "none" (no company identifier in the query).

signalType.description: Type of signal (always "funding_round" for this endpoint)

discoveredAt.description: When the signal was discovered by our system

companyName.description: Name of the company that received funding

companyLogo.description: URL to the company logo image

companyCountry.description: Country code where the company is headquartered

companySubcategory.description: Assigned company subcategory ID (same vocabulary as the `subcategories` query filter). Null when not classified.

companyDescription.example: dtcpay is a regulated payment service provider enabling seamless digital payments.

roundType.description: Funding round type (lowercase). Commonly one of the accepted `round` filter values (see the Enums page), but responses may include other, less common round types.

roundFlavor.description: Flavor/variant of the funding round (null for standard rounds)

amount.description: Funding amount in whole units of the deal's currency (not cents); see the Enums page for currency handling

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the funding amount (e.g. USD, EUR)

announcedDate.description: Date when the funding was announced (YYYY-MM-DD)

confidenceScore.description: AI confidence score for this signal (0.0 to 1.0)

verificationStatus.description: Verification status of the funding signal

isFreeAccess.description: Whether full data is accessible (always true for authenticated requests)

investors.description: List of investors participating in this round

sources.description: List of source articles and references for this signal

isLead.description: Whether this investor is the lead investor for this round

title.example: dtcpay raises $10M in Series A led by Sequoia Capital

isPrimary.description: Whether this is the primary source for the signal

totalCount.description: Total number of records matching the query

hasNextPage.description: Indicates if there is a next page

hasPreviousPage.description: Indicates if there is a previous page

creditsUsed.description: Number of API credits consumed by this request

creditsRemaining.description: Number of API credits remaining after this request

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful (always false for errors)

error.description: Error message describing what went wrong

signalId.description: Unique identifier for the acquisition signal

signalType.description: Type of signal (always "acquisition" for this endpoint)

match_confidence.description: How this row's company was matched: "exact" (via `company_domain` / `company_linkedin_url`), "fuzzy" (via `company_name` partial match), or "none" (no company identifier in the query).

companyId.description: Unique identifier for the acquired company

companySlug.description: URL-friendly slug for the acquired company

companyLogo.description: URL to the acquired company logo

companyCountry.description: Country code of the acquired company

companySubcategory.description: Assigned subcategory for the acquired company (same vocabulary as the `subcategories` filter)

companyFoundedYear.description: Year the acquired company was founded

companyEmployeeCount.description: Employee count of the acquired company

acquiringCompanyId.description: ID of the acquiring company (null if unknown)

amount.description: Acquisition amount in whole USD (not cents)

currency.description: Currency of the amount, as an ISO 4217 code. Usually "USD"; a few records are "EUR".

announcedDate.description: Date when the acquisition was announced

isFreeAccess.description: Whether full data is accessible (always true for authenticated requests)

acquiringCompany.description: Details of the acquiring company (null if unknown)

sources.description: Source articles and references for this signal

companySubcategory.description: Assigned subcategory (same vocabulary as the `subcategories` filter)

isPrimary.description: Whether this is the primary source

match_confidence.description: How this row's company was matched: "exact" (via `company_domain` / `company_linkedin_url`), "fuzzy" (via `company_name` partial match), or "none" (no company identifier in the query).

personName.description: Person's name (GDPR-masked: first name + last initial only)

postContent.description: Content of the post announcing the change

takenFrom.description: URL where the signal was sourced from

personCurrentTitle.description: Person's current title as shown on their profile

personPosts.description: JSON-encoded array of recent person posts

companySubcategory.description: Assigned company subcategory (same vocabulary as the `subcategories` filter)

isFreeAccess.description: Whether full data is accessible (always true for authenticated requests)

companyLogo.description: Alias of companyLogoUrl for backwards compatibility

id.description: Unique identifier for the hiring signal

match_confidence.description: How this row's company was matched: "exact" (via `company_domain` / `company_linkedin_url`), "fuzzy" (via `company_name` partial match), or "none" (no company identifier in the query).

jobId.description: External job ID from the source platform

jobCountry.description: Country code for the job listing location (not necessarily company HQ)

employmentType.description: Type of employment (e.g., Full-time, Part-time, Contract)

companySubcategory.description: Assigned company subcategory (same vocabulary as the `subcategories` filter)

isFreeAccess.description: Whether full data is accessible (always true for authenticated requests)

companyLogo.description: Alias of companyLogoUrl for backwards compatibility

name.description: Name of the investor or firm

countries.description: JSON-encoded array of country codes where the investor operates

createdAt.description: When the investor record was created

isFreeAccess.description: Whether full data is accessible (always true for authenticated requests)

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful

slug.description: URL-friendly unique slug for the company

description.example: Enterprise SaaS platform for workflow automation.

headquartersCountry.description: Country where the company is headquartered

growthInfo.description: Headcount growth percentages over various time periods

createdAt.description: When the company record was created

updatedAt.description: When the company record was last updated

totalCount.description: Total number of records matching the query

hasNextPage.description: Indicates if there is a next page

hasPreviousPage.description: Indicates if there is a previous page

creditsUsed.description: Number of API credits consumed by this request

creditsRemaining.description: API credits remaining on the team's balance after this request

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful (always false for errors)

error.description: Error message describing what went wrong

fileName.description: Optional label stored on the enrichment job. Defaults to api-companies.json.

wait.description: If true, wait up to timeout seconds and return completed results inline when available.

timeout.description: Maximum wait time in seconds when wait=true.

companies.description: Companies to enrich. Maximum 1,000 companies per request.

Csv_CompanyEnrichmentInput.description: Company input. Include at least one of company_name, website_url, or linkedin_url. Additional fields are preserved on the enrichment row.

retryAfter.description: Seconds until retry is recommended, present on rate limit responses.

Webhook_EventType.description: Event type a subscription can register for. system.test is accepted but only ever delivered by the Send Test Event endpoint; it never fires automatically.

Webhook_DeliveryStatus.description: Delivery lifecycle status. done = endpoint returned 2xx; failed = non-2xx or network error; pending = recorded but not yet resolved.

Webhook_Webhook.description: A webhook subscription. List/retrieve/update(no rotation)/delete include secret_preview; create and rotate_secret include the full secret instead.

event_types.description: Event types this subscription is registered for.

filters.description: Optional string key/value filters that narrow which events are delivered. Recognized keys: countries, categories, search, teamSize, dateFrom, dateTo, funding, roundFlavor, fundingAmount, acquisitionAmount, healthScore. Unrecognized keys are silently ignored — note the round-type key is `funding`, not `round`. See the Webhooks overview 'Filters' section for value formats.

active.description: Whether the webhook is active. Soft-deleted webhooks are active=false.

last_modified_by.description: ID of the user who last modified the webhook. Null if created before audit columns existed.

secret.description: Full HMAC signing secret (UUID). Returned ONLY on create and on update with rotate_secret:true.

secret_preview.description: An ellipsis followed by the last 4 characters of the secret (e.g. "…5a6b"). Returned on every read instead of the full secret.

id.description: Delivery ID. Pass to the retry endpoint as deliveryId.

event_id.description: Event ID, stable across the original delivery and any retries — use it to dedupe.

response_status.description: HTTP status the endpoint returned, or 0 on a network error, null if not recorded.

response_body.description: First 1000 characters of the endpoint's response body.

Webhook_WebhookCreateRequest.description: Field names accept snake_case (canonical) or camelCase (endpointUrl, eventTypes).

endpoint_url.description: Destination URL. Must be a valid http/https URL of at most 500 characters. In production, localhost and private/link-local addresses are rejected.

event_types.description: Event types to subscribe to. Duplicates are removed.

filters.description: Optional string key/value filters that narrow which events are delivered. Recognized keys: countries, categories, search, teamSize, dateFrom, dateTo, funding, roundFlavor, fundingAmount, acquisitionAmount, healthScore. Unrecognized keys are silently ignored — note the round-type key is `funding`, not `round`. See the Webhooks overview 'Filters' section for value formats.

Webhook_WebhookUpdateRequest.description: Send any subset of fields. Field names accept snake_case or camelCase (endpointUrl, eventTypes, rotateSecret).

endpoint_url.description: New destination URL. Same validation as create.

event_types.description: Replacement list of subscribed event types.

filters.description: Replacement filter map. Same recognized keys as on create (countries, categories, search, teamSize, dateFrom, dateTo, funding, roundFlavor, fundingAmount, acquisitionAmount, healthScore); unrecognized keys are ignored.

active.description: Set false to pause, or true to re-enable a soft-deleted webhook.

rotate_secret.description: If true, generate a new signing secret. The response includes the full new secret once; the old secret stops verifying immediately.

creditsUsed.description: API credits consumed by this request.

count.description: Number of items returned in this page.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page, or null when there are no more rows.

success.description: Whether the endpoint accepted the test event with a 2xx.

delivery_id.description: ID of the new delivery row created by this retry.

previous_delivery_id.description: ID of the delivery that was retried.

retryAfter.description: Seconds until a retry is recommended. Present on rate-limit (429) responses.
